---
layout: post
title:  "Automatic Transcription in Praat"
date:   2026-05-19 09:00:00 -0600
date-modified: last-modified
reference-location: margin
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
categories:
  - How-to Guides
  - Praat
number-sections: true
execute:
  eval: false
draft: true
---

I was on the Praat homepage and I noticed that [Anastasia Shchupak](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anastasiashch/) was added as an author to Praat. After a bit of digging, I saw on the [Acknowledgements](https://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/manual/Acknowledgments.html) page that she is responsiblle for automatic speech recognition with word-level timestamps, automatic speaker diarization, and adaptation of whisper.cpp, Silero VAD and pyannote.audio. Whoa. I hadn't heard that Praat can now do automatic transcription. So, I thought I'd put together a quick tutorial to see how to do that and to see how it works. 



Looks like you need to install Whisper. See documentation on how to do that here.

https://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/manual/TextGrid___Sound__Transcribe_interval___.html





Find preferences folder [here](https://praat.org/manual/preferences_folder.html). I had to manually create it.
